Now I understand the flow:1. The device is found in iwl_hw_card_ids based on device ID and PCI_ANY_ID for subdevice (catchall)2. The driver then looks through iwl_dev_info_table to find a more specific configuration based on device ID and subdevice ID\n3. If a match is found in iwl_dev_info_table, it uses that configuration\n4. If no match is found, it uses the configuration from iwl_hw_card_ids (which would be iwl9000_trans_cfg for the catchall)The problem is that for subdevices 0x4010, 0x4018, 0x401C, there's no match in iwl_dev_info_table, so they fall back to iwl9000_trans_cfg, which doesn't have the proper firmware configuration for the 9260.The solution is to add these three entries to iwl_dev_info_table with the configuration iwl9260_2ac_160_cfg and the name iwl9260_160_name.Now let me write the fault localization result.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":9,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1914,"cache_read_input_tokens":47830,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":1914},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"d509a9bc-f008-4d99-945c-5112f956d64a","uuid":"0587b05a-fb49-4b5d-ab26-e1c53637a0cf"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_01Qpor98gNwN9pxjBjvBMdAy","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01VZSWyuTWec8iUChtpBq8i7","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/workspace/fault_localization_result.json","content":"{\n  \"buggy_files\": [\"drivers/net/wireless/intel/iwlwifi/pcie/drv.c\"],\n  \"buggy_functions\": [\"iwl_dev_info_table\"],\n  \"confidence\": 0.95,\n  \"reasoning\": \"The Intel Wireless-AC 9260 (device 0x2526) is missing PCI subdevice entries for 0x4010, 0x4018, and 0x401C in the iwl_dev_info_table. These subdevices lack explicit device information entries, causing the driver to fall back to the generic iwl9000_trans_cfg configuration from the catchall entry in iwl_hw_card_ids, which does not provide the correct firmware configuration for the 9260 card. This results in firmware loading failures. The fix requires adding three new IWL_DEV_INFO entries to iwl_dev_info_table for device 0x2526 with subdevices 0x4010, 0x4018, and 0x401C, mapping them to iwl9260_2ac_160_cfg (160MHz variant) and iwl9260_160_name, similar to existing entries for prefixes 0x, 0x6, 0x8, 0xA, and 0xE with the same suffixes.\"\n}\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":9,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1914,"cache_read_input_tokens":47830,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":1914},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"d509a9bc-f008-4d99-945c-5112f956d64a","uuid":"b369fb1a-dd67-4e9c-a1f9-25c92af84134"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_01HwwWLonTUhejH9C2mBahtz","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Perfect! I have completed my fault localization analysis. Let me summarize my findings:\n\n## Fault Localization Summary\n\n**File**: `drivers/net/wireless/intel/iwlwifi/pcie/drv.c`\n\n**Data Structure**: `iwl_dev_info_table` (static const array)\n\n**Issue**: The Intel Wireless-AC 9260 card (device ID 0x2526) is missing device information entries for three PCI subdevice IDs: **0x4010**, **0x4018**, and **0x401C** in the `iwl_dev_info_table`.\n\n**Root Cause**: When these subdevices are not found in `iwl_dev_info_table`, the driver falls back to the generic configuration `iwl9000_trans_cfg` from the catchall entry in `iwl_hw_card_ids`. This generic configuration does not provide the correct firmware settings for the Wireless-AC 9260, resulting in firmware loading failures.\n\n**Solution**: Add three new `IWL_DEV_INFO` entries to the `iwl_dev_info_table` for device 0x2526:\n- `IWL_DEV_INFO(0x2526, 0x4010, iwl9260_2ac_160_cfg, iwl9260_160_name)`\n- `IWL_DEV_INFO(0x2526, 0x4018, iwl9260_2ac_160_cfg, iwl9260_160_name)`\n- `IWL_DEV_INFO(0x2526, 0x401C, iwl9260_2ac_160_cfg, iwl9260_160_name)`\n\nThese entries follow the same pattern as existing 9260 160MHz entries with different prefixes (0x0, 0x6, 0x8, 0xA, 0xE).\n\n**Result written to**: `/workspace/fault_localization_result.json`"}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":8,"cache_creation_input_tokens":542,"cache_read_input_tokens":52666,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":542},"output_tokens":8,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"d509a9bc-f008-4d99-945c-5112f956d64a","uuid":"0a772929-653d-4855-a237-d24cd7db0a70"}
